name: Registry Link List
description: Compact list of registry destinations, documentation references, namespaces, or related content links. Optimized for sidebars, detail pages, and post-hero navigation blocks.

compact.description: Single-column link list with icon, title, short helper text, and optional count or status badge.

two-column.description: Two-column link inventory for larger registry overview sections.

slots.link-item: Full row link with icon, title, helper copy, and trailing affordance.

visual_brief: A utilitarian but polished registry navigation list. It should feel like a trustworthy map of related destinations, not a decorative card grid. Rows should be easy to scan, with clear link affordances, subtle dividers or hover states, and enough helper copy to explain why the destination matters.

responsive.mobile: Single-column full-width rows with titles and helper copy wrapping cleanly.
responsive.tablet: Single or two-column layout depending on available width.
responsive.desktop: May use two columns or a sidebar list. Keep row height consistent.

layout_hints.row_density: Keep rows compact. Avoid card-heavy treatment unless each link has substantial context.
layout_hints.trailing_affordance: Use an arrow, external-link icon, or subtle badge so links are unmistakable.
layout_hints.metadata: Counts and namespaces belong in a trailing badge, not mixed into the title.

motion.micro: Rows may brighten or slide their trailing icon a few pixels on hover.
motion.transitions: Filter updates should preserve list position and avoid dramatic reflow.

keyboard: Each row should expose one clear link target with a descriptive accessible name.
announcements: Destination title and helper copy should be included in the accessible label or nearby description.
accessibility.focus_management: Focused rows should show an obvious outline or background shift.
